name: submit_coaching
description: Submit your coaching analysis for the just-completed run. Call this tool exactly once. Do not respond in free text — the tool call IS your response.

run_type.description: Classify this run from the fixed taxonomy. Infer from pace, HR distribution, and structure.

load_rating.description: Overall stress of this run, accounting for duration, intensity, terrain, and recent fatigue.

flags.description: Short concern TAGS (not sentences) — 2-6 words each, ≤120 chars. Empty array if nothing notable. Good: 'high cardiac drift', 'pace fade km6+', '3rd hard session this week'. Bad: full sentences explaining the concern (use to_watch for that).

run_summary.description: Factual recap of the run: distance, pace, HR distribution, elevation, anything notable. 2–5 sentences. Plain text, no markdown.

went_well.description: What the runner did well. Pacing, HR control, executing the planned workout, building from a previous run, etc. 2–5 sentences. Plain text.

to_watch.description: Concerns: fatigue accumulation, HR drift, positive splits, overreach signals, injury risk indicators. 2–5 sentences. If there are no concerns, write a short 'nothing to flag' note. Plain text.

digest.description: Single-sentence summary suitable for the /history one-line view. Combines the most important insight from went_well and to_watch.

scheduled_date.description: ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) in the runner's local timezone. MUST be strictly in the future and MUST fall on one of the runner's available_days.

relative_offset_days.description: Number of days from today to scheduled_date. e.g. 3 = 'in 3 days'.

distance_km.description: Total session distance in km, including warmup and cooldown.

target_pace_min_km.description: Target average pace for the main effort, in minutes per km (float). e.g. 4.5 = 4:30/km.

target_pace_label.description: Display string for the pace, e.g. '4:30/km' or a range like '4:25/km–4:35/km'. Renders directly in the Telegram message.

target_hr_zone.description: Target HR zone for the main effort. Single zone (e.g. 'Z2') or a range (e.g. 'Z2-Z3', 'Z1-Z2', 'Z3-Z5'). Z1 is recovery, Z5 is VO2max. Use a range only when the workout legitimately spans zones.

warmup.description: Warmup description. e.g. '2km easy @ 6:00/km'. Empty string for easy/recovery runs that have no structured warmup.

main.description: Main effort description. e.g. '6×800m @ 4:00/km w/ 90s jog rest' or '8km easy @ 5:45–6:00/km, Z2'. Complex sessions like progression runs or fartleks may be longer.

cooldown.description: Cooldown description. e.g. '2km easy @ 6:00/km'. Empty string for easy/recovery runs.

notes.description: Optional freeform note (≤600 chars, 1–4 sentences). Use to explain the reasoning behind the recommendation, acknowledge an injury, flag recent fatigue, or note how this session fits the weekly budget. Empty string if nothing to add.
